sourceMovie.title: Allegiant
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Allegiant for the same reason it broke through in 2016: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a science fiction rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Robert Schwentke keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The adventure and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Oblivion
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Allegiant's adventure stayed with you, Oblivion (2013) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The science-fiction through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: The Hunger Games: Catching Fire
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Allegiant excels in adventure, The Hunger Games: Catching Fire (2013) doubles down on science-fiction to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Divergent
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Divergent (2014) is the natural next step after Allegiant: same adventure DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and science-fiction elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Allegiant who respond to its adventure will find The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 2 (2015) immediately familiar. Both films share a science-fiction sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: 2001: A Space Odyssey
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Allegiant fans keep landing on 2001: A Space Odyssey (1968) — the adventure is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials draws from the same adventure well as Allegiant, channeling science-fiction in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2015 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: What Happened to Monday
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: What Happened to Monday (2017) captures the science-fiction appeal of Allegiant without simply imitating it. The action streak running through both films is what puts What Happened to Monday at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: The Host
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The adventure appeal of Allegiant — that tense edge, the science-fiction — translates cleanly into The Host (2013). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Tron
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Tron (1982) clearly understood what makes adventure work — the same quality that keeps Allegiant rewatchable. Expect the same tense rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring (2001) is the kind of film Allegiant fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The adventure overlap is real, but it's the tense emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

faq.question: What should I watch after Allegiant?
faq.answer: Try Oblivion, The Hunger Games: Catching Fire, Divergent—each shares DNA with Allegiant while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Allegiant?
faq.answer: Allegiant (2016) is best described as Adventure, Science Fiction, Action, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Allegiant part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Divergent Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
